title: cephalotaxus harringtonia var. koreana
intro: Cephalotaxus harringtonia var. koreana, also known as the Korean plum yew, is a variety of coniferous plant native to Korea and parts of China, belonging to the Taxaceae family. The plant has been found to contain taxine, a toxic alkaloid with medicinal properties, particularly in treating certain types of cancer and neurodegenerative diseases. The health benefits of Cephalotaxus harringtonia var. koreana include its potential to inhibit the growth of cancer cells, reduce inflammation, and exhibit neuroprotective effects. In traditional Korean medicine, the plant is prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or extracts, often combined with other medicinal plants, to be used for treating various health conditions.
